headline: SMI
summary: closed at **13,136** (+0.80%) on Thursday 30 April, recovering into month-end.

headline: DAX
summary: closed at **24,292** (+1.4%) on Thursday 30 April, snapping an eight-day losing streak and booking a **+7.1% monthly gain** — its strongest April in years.

headline: CAC 40
summary: closed at **8,109** (+0.46%) on Thursday 30 April, closing out a positive month across continental Europe.

headline: FTSE 100
summary: closed at **10,363.90** (-0.14%) on Friday 1 May, the only major European bourse open that day.

headline: S&P 500
summary: closed at **7,230.12** (+0.29%) on Friday 1 May — a **new all-time closing record**, first close above 7,200.

headline: Nasdaq
summary: closed at **25,114.44** (+0.89%) on Friday 1 May, also an **all-time record**, led by Apple (+3%+ post-earnings).

headline: Dow Jones
summary: closed at **49,499.27** (-0.31%) on Friday 1 May, underperforming as Apple is not a Dow component and industrial names dragged.

headline: EUR/CHF: 0.9172
summary: — franc in demand as a safe haven through the Iran war ceasefire uncertainty; the pair has drifted below parity for an extended stretch.

headline: USD/CHF: 0.7815
summary: (intra-week range: 0.7789–0.7921 over Apr 30–May 1) — dollar retreated sharply against the franc, consistent with a broader USD softening as Iran ceasefire talks reduced some geopolitical risk premium on the dollar.

headline: ECB held rates unchanged
summary: at the 30 April Governing Council meeting: deposit facility **2.00%**, main refi **2.15%**.

headline: Bank of England held at 3.75%
summary: (30 April, 8-1 vote).

headline: Fed held at 3.50–3.75%
summary: (29 April).

headline: Crude oil (WTI): above $105/barrel
summary: at Friday's close — second consecutive weekly gain.

headline: Gold: $4,614.98/oz
summary: spot (-0.1% Friday), logging a weekly loss despite high geopolitical risk.

headline: Copper: ~$5.90/lb
summary: futures, steadying after five consecutive losing sessions.

headline: Apple Q2 FY2026
summary: (reported 30 April, after-hours): Revenue **$111.2B** (+17% YoY), EPS **$2.01** (+22% YoY) — both ahead of consensus ($109.66B and $1.95).

headline: Trump declares Iran war "terminated" — War Powers clock dispute intensifies.
summary: In a 1 May letter to Congress, Trump wrote that "hostilities that began on 28 February 2026, have terminated," arguing the 60-day War Powers Resolution clock is paused by the nominal ceasefire and thus no congressional authorization is needed.

headline: Iran submits 14-point counter-proposal; first Strait attack in weeks reported.
summary: Tehran submitted a detailed counter-proposal to the US peace framework on 2 May; Trump said Saturday he is "reviewing it." Iranian foreign ministry said Sunday it is in turn "reviewing the latest US reply," per CNN live updates.

headline: Trump announces 25% tariff on EU cars and trucks.
summary: On Friday 1 May, Trump said he is raising tariffs on EU-made vehicles to **25%** — breaching the 15% ceiling agreed in the July 2025 US-EU trade deal — citing EU non-compliance.

headline: Kashkari: Iran war has "changed" the Fed rate outlook.
summary: Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari appeared on CBS *Face the Nation* this morning and said the conflict "limits the Fed's ability to provide rate guidance."
